metadata.name: High-Availability Q&A
metadata.description: Multi-model fallback question answering using ResilientAgent
metadata.author: Kaizen Team

inputs.name: Question
inputs.description: Question to answer with fallback support
inputs.default: What are the key principles of microservices architecture?

outputs.name: Fallback Chain
outputs.description: List of models attempted before success

documentation.overview: This workflow provides high-availability question answering by automatically falling back through a chain of models if the primary model fails. It tries GPT-4 first, falls back to GPT-3.5-turbo if unavailable, and finally uses a local model as the last resort. Perfect for production systems requiring guaranteed uptime.
use_cases: Cost optimization by trying expensive models first, falling back to cheaper ones
Provider redundancy to avoid vendor lock-in
Progressive degradation for graceful failure handling
prerequisites: OpenAI API key configured as OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
Internet connection for cloud API access

example_inputs.question: What are the key principles of microservices architecture?
Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms
What are the benefits of serverless computing?

example_outputs.answer: Key principles of microservices architecture include: 1) Single Responsibility - each service does one thing well, 2) Independence - services can be deployed and scaled independently, 3) Decentralization - distributed data and governance, 4) API-first - well-defined service interfaces, 5) Resilience - design for failure.
Microservices are small, independent services that communicate via APIs...
